summary: Blinded judge evaluation strips condition identity and coordination artifacts from LLM judge context
context: External research review identified artifact leakage as a major credibility risk — judges seeing coordination files could reward documentation quality over engineering quality, biasing results toward structured coordination systems.
rationale: blindMode strips .twining/ paths, COORDINATION.md, CONTEXT.md, coordination/ dirs, and twining_* tool names from diffs. Coordination artifacts set to empty string. Standalone quality evaluation always uses blind mode. Coordination rubrics use semi-blind (artifacts present, identity stripped).

alternatives.option: Only blind standalone rubrics, leave coordination rubrics fully unblinded
alternatives.reason_rejected: Even coordination rubrics should not know which condition produced the output — knowing the condition name could bias the judge toward expected outcomes
